title: Workato Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Workato** is an enterprise iPaaS / automation platform exposing a REST API at `https://www.workato.com/api` (US) or `https://app.eu.workato.com/api` (EU). This connector — `WorkatoConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "workato"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Workato workspace:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Recipes | `/recipes` | List, get, start, stop integration recipes |
| Connections | `/connections` | List, get, create connector connections |
| Folders | `/folders` | Project / folder navigation |
| Jobs | `/recipes/{id}/jobs` | Recipe job runs (audit + retry surface) |
| Lookup Tables | `/lookup_tables` | Configurable runtime data |
| Tags | `/tags` | Resource tagging |
| Users | `/users` | Workspace users |
| On-Prem Agents | `/on_prem_agents` | OPA fleet inventory |
| Customers | `/managed_users` | White-label / embedded customer accounts |

The connector normalises recipes + connections + jobs into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{connector_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::WorkatoHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Workato API token

1. Sign in to https://www.workato.com (or https://app.eu.workato.com for EU).
2. Open **Workspace Admin → API Clients** (left nav).
3. Click **Create client** → select scopes (Recipes, Connections, Jobs, etc.) → **Generate Token**.
4. Copy the token value — Workato shows it only once.
5. Note the data center you used (US or EU) — you'll set the `region` install field accordingly.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Workato**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_token` (secret) | Token from step 4 |
| `region` (string, optional) | `us` (default) or `eu` |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | Explicit override — leave blank for the region default |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 100 |
| `timeout_s` (number) | HTTP timeout in seconds, default 30 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_token` is present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `WorkatoHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which calls `GET /users/me` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Workato REST API uses **server-to-server API token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry — tokens live until rotated in Workato Admin.

### Credential model

- `api_token` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <api_token>`.
- `region` selects the base URL (`us` → `https://www.workato.com/api`, `eu` → `https://app.eu.workato.com/api`).
- `base_url` can override the resolved region URL for sandbox / proxy deployments.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_token`. Initialises `WorkatoHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Workato endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a TokenInfo wrapping the `api_token` — there is no exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /users/me` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <api_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_token` | secret | yes | `self.api_token` | Bearer token in Authorization header |
| `region` | string | no | `self.region` (default `us`) | Selects US or EU base URL |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults from region) | Override the resolved base URL |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 100) | Client-side soft cap |
| `timeout_s` | number | no | `self.timeout_s` (default 30) | Per-request httpx timeout |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
CONNECTOR_TYPE = "workato"
AUTH_TYPE      = "api_key"
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_WORKATO_BASE_US = "https://www.workato.com/api"
_WORKATO_BASE_EU = "https://app.eu.workato.com/api"
```

### Region resolution

The HTTP client's `_region_to_base_url(region)` resolves:
- `"us"` (default) → `https://www.workato.com/api`
- `"eu"` → `https://app.eu.workato.com/api`
- explicit `base_url` install_field — wins over `region` when set.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `WorkatoConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::WorkatoHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_token`; initialise `WorkatoHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a TokenInfo wrapping `api_token`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Calls `GET /users/me`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Workato recipes, connections, and recent jobs per recipe → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Recipes
children.content:
### `async list_recipes(*, page=1, per_page=100, folder_id=None, order=None) -> Dict`
`GET /recipes?page={N}&per_page={M}&folder_id={F}&order={O}`.

### `async get_recipe(recipe_id) -> Dict`
`GET /recipes/{recipe_id}`.

### `async start_recipe(recipe_id) -> Dict`
`PUT /recipes/{recipe_id}/start`.

### `async stop_recipe(recipe_id) -> Dict`
`PUT /recipes/{recipe_id}/stop`.

children.title: Connections
children.content:
### `async list_connections(*, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /connections`.

### `async get_connection(connection_id) -> Dict`
`GET /connections/{connection_id}`.

### `async create_connection(payload) -> Dict`
`POST /connections` — body is the raw connection object.

children.title: Folders + Jobs
children.content:
### `async list_folders(*, page=1, per_page=100, parent_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /folders`.

### `async list_jobs(recipe_id, *, page=1, per_page=100, status=None) -> Dict`
`GET /recipes/{recipe_id}/jobs`.

### `async get_job(recipe_id, job_id) -> Dict`
`GET /recipes/{recipe_id}/jobs/{job_id}`.

children.title: Lookup Tables · Tags · Users · OPA · Customers
children.content:
### `async list_lookup_tables(*, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /lookup_tables`.

### `async list_tags(*, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /tags`.

### `async list_users(*, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /users`.

### `async list_on_prem_agents(*, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /on_prem_agents`.

### `async list_customers(*, page=1, per_page=100) -> Dict`
`GET /managed_users` — embedded / white-label customer accounts.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
WorkatoError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── WorkatoAuthError                  # 401 / 403
├── WorkatoBadRequestError            # 400
├── WorkatoNotFoundError              # 404
├── WorkatoConflictError              # 409
├── WorkatoRateLimitError             # 429 — retry_after_s
└── WorkatoServerError                # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
WorkatoNetworkError = WorkatoServerError      # legacy name from older code
WorkatoNotFound     = WorkatoNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s), retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `WorkatoNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_token` blank | Re-enter the token in ACP. Workato shows the token only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Workato — API token lacks the workspace role / scope | In Workato → Workspace Admin → API Clients, edit the client and grant the recipes/connections/jobs read scopes (whichever surfaces you use). |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — token revoked or wrong region | Verify `region` matches the data center that issued the token. Rotate the token if needed. |
| `workato.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Workato per-minute rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on every call | Wrong `region` — calls hit the US base URL when the token is EU (or vice versa) | Set `region="eu"` (or `region="us"`) in the install form so the client targets the right data center. |
| Calls land at `https://www.workato.com/api` but token is EU | `region` install_field empty/`us` and `base_url` not set | Set `region="eu"`; the client resolves to `https://app.eu.workato.com/api`. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'WorkatoNetworkError'` | Older exceptions.py without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `WorkatoNetworkError = WorkatoServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
